Name: Pattern Recognition - PE

Text: Case 1 OR to MS to QS to AS to IQC to AS to TST to RT to TST to PK to LB to DP to D Case 2 OR to MS to QS to AS to IQC to AS to TST to PK to DP to D Case 3 OR to MS to QS to AS to IQC to IQC to AS to IQC to AS to TST to RT to TST to PK to LB to DP to D Case 4 OR to MS to QS to AS to TST to PK to LB to DP to D Case 5 OR to MS to QS to AS to IQC to AS to IQC to AS to TST to RT to TST to RT to TST to PK to LB to DP to D

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: This is clearly a loop TST to RT to TST and TST to RT to TST to RT to TST This is clearly a loop execute TST then either exit or RT then TST again

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The loop operator in POWL is star A B meaning execute A then either exit or execute B and then A again So star TST RT generates TST then RT TST repeated and matches all cases

Name: Validation - PE

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND

Name: Validation - NE
Text: Alternative star AS IQC expects AS first after the segment starts but we have IQC first so this does not fit

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Lets reconsider Case 3 IQC IQC AS IQC AS Maybe the loop children can themselves be complex POWL models

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Since the last element before TST is always AS the outer loop should have A equal to AS because star A B produces traces ending with A before exit

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: What if B is itself a loop and B equals star IQC Silent so B generates one or more IQC events

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Outer Loop star AS B executes AS then either exits or executes B then AS again If B equals star IQC Silent then B generates IQC one or more times
